page_content:
PSYC440 Experimental Psychology: Cognitive Processes and Legal Applications (4 Credits) A survey of the content, models, and methods in cognitive psychology with an emphasis on attention and encoding, recall, recognition, judgment, signal detection theory, and applying cognitive theories to situations in the legal system. Students integrate scientiﬁc theories with real-life legal situations. Course topics include research methodology in assessing and addressing cognitive mechanisms and how this understanding may help eyewitness and victim recall and recognition, perpetrator recall, assessing scientiﬁc theories of repression, and real life examples. Prerequisite: PSYC100, PSYC200, PSYC300 and PSYC341. Restriction: Must be in Psychology program or Social Data Science - Psychology Track; and must have earned a minimum of 85 credits.

PSYC432 Counseling Psychology: Theories, Research, and Practice (3 Credits) Analysis of research and intervention strategies developed and used by counseling psychologists. Historical and current trends in content and methodology. Prerequisite: PSYC200.
